# Copilot Instructions

## First-run check

**Before doing anything else**, check whether `blazorade.config.md` exists at the repository root.

- **If it does not exist:** read `.github/instructions/first-run.instructions.md` and follow it completely before proceeding with anything else. Do not perform any other work until first-run has completed and `blazorade.config.md` has been written.
- **If it exists:** read it to load the site configuration (display name, app name, host name, project paths), then continue with the rest of these instructions.

## Project overview

This is the source repository for a static Blazor WebAssembly website built with Blazorade AI Publisher. The site identity and project structure are defined in `blazorade.config.md` at the repository root.

Content is authored as Markdown files in `/content`, published to static HTML bootstrappers directly in `{WebAppPath}/wwwroot/`, and served by a Blazor WASM app that fetches and renders each page at runtime.

## Repository structure

The paths below use `{WebAppPath}` and `{ComponentLibraryPath}` as defined in `blazorade.config.md`.

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `content/` | Markdown source files with YAML frontmatter. Edit these to update site content. |
| `{WebAppPath}/` | Blazor WASM application (pages, layout, routing). |
| `{ComponentLibraryPath}/` | Reusable Razor component library referenced by the web app. |
| `{WebAppPath}/wwwroot/{slug}.html` | Published static HTML bootstrappers — do not edit manually, regenerated by publish. |
| `{WebAppPath}/wwwroot/sitemap.xml` | Generated sitemap — do not edit manually, regenerated by publish. |
| `{WebAppPath}/wwwroot/staticwebapp.config.json` | Generated route rewrites — do not edit manually, regenerated by publish. |
| `{WebAppPath}/Layout/NavMenu.razor` | Top navbar — do not edit manually, regenerated by publish. |
| `.github/instructions/` | Instruction files loaded by Copilot for specific areas of the codebase. |

## Publishing

When the user asks to publish content, follow the [publishing instructions](./instructions/publish.instructions.md).

## Confirmation before making changes

**Never apply changes to the codebase without explicit confirmation from the user.**

A message is treated as a question — and therefore not a directive to implement — when **any** of the following are true:

- It contains a question mark (`?`).
- It is phrased interrogatively, even without a question mark (e.g. *"Are you ready to implement"*, *"Can you do that"*, *"Would that work"*).
- It asks for an opinion, suggestion, or plan, or uses phrasing like *"what would you do"*, *"how would you"*, *"what do you think about"*, *"could you"*, *"would it be possible to"*.

When the message is a question: respond with an answer, opinion, or proposal only. Do not write or modify any files.

Only proceed with edits when the user has given an unambiguous, non-interrogative directive, e.g. *"go ahead"*, *"implement this"*, *"make that change"*, *"do it"*, or similar.

When in doubt, always ask first.

## Todo documents

The `/todo` folder is a lightweight task-tracking system for ongoing and future work.

- `/todo/home.md` is the **index** — a table with one row per task, each row containing a link to the detail document and a one-sentence description. Do not put task detail here.
- Each task has its own detail document (e.g. `/todo/reusable-ai-site-builder.md`) containing the full context, decisions made, and next steps.
- When a new task is identified: create a detail document in `/todo/` and add a row to `/todo/home.md`.
- When a task is fully complete: remove its row from `/todo/home.md` and delete its detail document.
- Never load all detail documents speculatively. Read `/todo/home.md` to get an overview, then read a specific detail document only when that task is actively being worked on.

## Key conventions

- **Never edit** generated files directly (`wwwroot/{slug}.html`, `sitemap.xml`, `staticwebapp.config.json`, `NavMenu.razor`). Always edit the source markdown and run publish.
- **All reusable UI components** belong in `{ComponentLibraryPath}`. Do not add one-off markup to the web app pages without checking there first.
- **Content pages** in the web app load their HTML by fetching the corresponding `.html` bootstrapper and extracting the `<main>` element via the JS helper in `wwwroot/js/`. Head metadata is applied via Blazor's `<HeadContent>`.
- **Navigation** is a top navbar with no left sidebar. Flat pages get a direct link; subdirectories under `wwwroot/` get a dropdown whose label comes from the subdirectory's `home.md` frontmatter title.
